title: JavaScript Dialog Boxes Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with dialogs and messages.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in JavaScript for getting the user's attention?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using built-in dialogs (alert, confirm, prompt) to show simple interactive messages.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using confirm() compared to alert()?
options: It makes your HTML files smaller and more readable.
It provides 'OK' and 'Cancel' buttons (returning true or false), allowing your code to branch logic based on the user's choice.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'alert()' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
To show a single message with an 'OK' button to the user.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' everywhere in your professional JavaScript dialogs?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can make the site load much slower and potentially confuse the dialog logic.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for JavaScript dialog boxes?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using 'confirm()' for high-stakes actions (like 'Delete Account') to provide a double-check for the user.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
